draft.introduction: Psalm 145 is an acrostic hymn of praise declaring that God’s kingdom endures through all generations. It is the only psalm with the superscription “praise” (tehillah).

translation.text: I will extol you, my God and King, and bless your name forever and ever. Every day I will bless you and praise your name forever and ever. Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised, and his greatness is unsearchable.
translation.original_text: ὑψώσω σε ὁ θεός μου ὁ βασιλεύς

translation.text: The Lord is gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love. The Lord is good to all, and his mercy is over all that he has made. Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and your dominion endures throughout all generations.

translator_notes.note: The everlasting kingdom anticipates Daniel’s vision of the eternal kingdom given to the “son of man.” Jewish and Christian eschatology both draw on this language of divine sovereignty without end.
